page: OVHcloud r3-32 is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz, with one thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 100 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench text processing also demonstrate strong performance. Multi-core metrics, including stress-ng multi-core CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth is average, reaching 120.0 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 74.19 GB/sec for large block write operations.

While single-core execution is strong, the instance exhibits weaker performance in static web serving throughput and prompt processing for medium-sized (7B) language models, both of which rank in the bottom 25%. However, its high memory density relative to CPU resources and average database operations performance make it suitable for memory-bound workloads, caching layers such as Redis (which achieved 9.91 million SET operations per second), and lightweight database hosting.
description: OVHcloud r3-32 is a memory-optimized virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan architecture. It is configured with 4 dedicated physical cores running at 2.3 GHz, 32.0 GB of system memory, and a bundled 100 GB NVMe SSD. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%, alongside strong single-threaded text processing and decompression. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the average tier. However, static web serving throughput and medium-model LLM prompt processing are weak, placing in the bottom 25%. This resource profile, characterized by a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, is suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers like Redis, and database operations rather than high-throughput web serving.
og_description: OVHcloud r3-32 is a memory-optimized KVM instance with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 100 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud r3-32 is a memory-optimized KVM instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 100 GB NVMe SSD.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high memory density, and local NVMe storage for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores running at 2.3 GHz
32 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio
100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 1 Gbps baseline bandwidth
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth metrics
Suited for caching layers, database operations, and memory-bound tasks
